name: NurbsCurvePointList
summary: Provides access to the control points of a NURBS curve.

methods.name: ChangeEndWeights
methods.signature: public bool ChangeEndWeights(double w0, double w1)
methods.summary: Use a combination of scaling and reparameterization to change the end weights to the specified values.
methods.remarks: The domain, Euclidean locations of the control points, and locus of the curve do not change, but the weights, homogeneous CV values and internal knot values may change. If w0 and w1 are 1 and the curve is not rational, the curve is not changed.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: Contains
methods.signature: public bool Contains(ControlPoint item)
methods.summary: Determines if this list contains an item.

params.name: item
params.doc: The exact item to search for.

methods.name: ControlPolygon
methods.signature: public Polyline ControlPolygon()
methods.summary: Constructs a polyline through all the control points. Note that periodic curves generate a closed polyline with fewer points than control-points.

returns.doc: A polyline connecting all control points.

methods.name: CopyTo
methods.signature: public void CopyTo(ControlPoint[] array, int arrayIndex)
methods.summary: Copied the list to an array.

params.name: arrayIndex
params.doc: The index in which the copy will begin.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.signature: public void EnsurePrivateCopy()
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(NurbsCurvePointList other, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

methods.name: GetPoint(Int32, Point3d)
methods.signature: public bool GetPoint(int index, out Point3d point)
methods.summary: Gets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point at the given index. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to get.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetPoint(Int32, Point4d)
methods.signature: public bool GetPoint(int index, out Point4d point)
methods.summary: Gets a homogeneous control point at the given index, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
methods.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use the override that returns world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to get.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinate and weight of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: GetWeight
methods.signature: public double GetWeight(int index)
methods.summary: Gets the weight of a control point at the given index. Note, if the curve is non-rational, the weight will be 1.0.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to get.

returns.doc: The control point weight if successful, Rhino.Math.UnsetValue otherwise.

methods.name: IndexOf
methods.signature: public int IndexOf(ControlPoint item)
methods.summary: Gets the index of a control point, or -1.

params.name: item
params.doc: The exact item to search for.

methods.name: MakeNonRational
methods.signature: public bool MakeNonRational()
methods.summary: Converts the curve to a Non-rational NURBS curve. Non-rational curves have unweighted control points.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: MakeRational
methods.signature: public bool MakeRational()
methods.summary: Converts the curve to a Rational NURBS curve. Rational NURBS curves have weighted control points.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Point3d)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int index, Point3d point)
methods.summary: Sets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point at the given index. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to set.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Point4d)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int index, Point4d point)
methods.summary: Sets a homogeneous control point at the given index, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
methods.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use an override that accepts world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates as input.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to set.

params.name: point
params.doc: Coordinate and weight of control point.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Point3d, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int index, Point3d point, double weight)
methods.summary: Sets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point and weight at a given index. The 4-D representation is (x*w, y*w, z*w, w).

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to set.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int index, double x, double y, double z)
methods.summary: Sets a world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point at the given index. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to set.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetPoint(Int32, Double, Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool SetPoint(int index, double x, double y, double z, double weight)
methods.summary: Sets a homogeneous control point at the given index, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
methods.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use an override that accepts world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates as input.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to set.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: SetWeight
methods.signature: public bool SetWeight(int index, double weight)
methods.summary: Sets the weight of a control point at the given index Note, if the curve is non-rational, it will be converted to rational.

params.name: index
params.doc: Index of control point to set.

methods.name: UVNDirectionsAt
methods.signature: public bool UVNDirectionsAt(int index, out Vector3d uDir, out Vector3d vDir, out Vector3d nDir)
methods.summary: Calculates the U, V, and N directions of a NURBS curve control point similar to the method used by Rhino's MoveUVN command.

methods.name: ValidateSpacing
methods.signature: public bool ValidateSpacing(double closeTolerance, double stackTolerance, out int[] closeIndices, out int[] stackedIndices)
methods.summary: Simple check of distance between adjacent control points

params.name: closeTolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use for determining if control points are 'close'

params.name: stackTolerance
params.doc: tolerance to use for determining if control points are 'stacked'

params.name: closeIndices
params.doc: indices of 'close' points are returned in this array

params.name: stackedIndices
params.doc: indices of 'stacked' points are returned in this array

returns.doc: true if close or stacked indices are found

properties.name: ControlPolygonLength
properties.summary: Gets the length of the polyline connecting all control points.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Gets the number of control points in this curve.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets the control point location at the given index.

properties.name: PointSize
properties.summary: Returns the control point size, or the number of doubles per control point. For rational curves, PointSize = Curve.Dimension + 1. For non-rational curves, PointSize = Curve.Dimension.
